perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) calendar days of written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return to the Disclosing Party, or at the Disclosing Party's option, shall destroy, any physical and written records containing such Confidential Information, whether such physical and written records were sup plied or made available by the Disclosing Party or were prepared by the R ecipient or its Representatives from information sup plied or made available by the Disclosing Party. In such event, the R ecipient shall certify in writing to the Disclosing P arty that such physical or written materials have been returned o r destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Within one-hundred and eighty (180) calendar days of written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party may, but is not obligated to return to the Disclosing Party, or at the Receiving Party's option, shall retain, any physical and written records containing such Confidential Information, whether such physical and written records were sup plied or made available by the Disclosing Party or were prepared by the R ecipient or its Representatives from information sup plied or made available by the Disclosing Party. In such event, the R ecipient is not obligated to certify in writing to the Disclosing P arty that such physical or written materials have been returned o r destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'ten (10) calendar days' to 'one-hundred and eighty (180) calendar days', 'shall return' to 'may, but is not obligated to return' and 'at the Disclosing Party's option, shall destroy' to 'at the Receiving Party's option, shall retain', 'Recipient shall certify' to 'Recipient is not obligated to certify' introduces a contradiction in the nature and term of the original agreement. The Receiving party is given full discretion over retaining and returning confidential information which compromises the terms of this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Return . Within one-hundred and eighty (180) calendar days of written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party may, but is not obligated to return to the Disclosing Party, or at the Receiving Party's option, shall retain, any physical and written records containing such Confidential Information, whether such physical and written records were sup plied or made available by the Disclosing Party or were prepared by the R ecipient or its Representatives from information sup plied or made available by the Disclosing Party. In such event, the R ecipient is not obligated to certify in writing to the Disclosing P arty that such physical or written materials have been returned o r destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the obligations of the Receiving Party regarding confidential information. The original text mandates the return or destruction of confidential information within ten days, while the changed text allows the Receiving Party to retain the information at its discretion, and removes the obligation to certify its return or destruction. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, as the changed text makes the return of confidential information optional, creating a direct conflict in the document concerning the handling of confidential information, and is categorized as an Ambiguity - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party understands and acknowledges that no representation or warranty of any kind, wh ether express or implied, is made as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information provid ed by either Party, and neither the Disclosing P arty nor any of its affiliates, Representatives, directors, officers, equity owners, managers, employees or agents s hall have any liability to the R ecipient or to any other person or en tity resulting from use of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party understands and acknowledges that a representation or warranty of any kind, wh ether express or implied, is made as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information provid ed by either Party, and the Disclosing P arty and its affiliates, Representatives, directors, officers, equity owners, managers, employees or agents s hall have liability to the R ecipient or to any other person or en tity resulting from use of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'no' to 'a', and changing 'neither' to 'the' directly contradicts the original meaning and places the accountability on the Disclosing party. This introduces in-text contradiction to the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. No Representation . Each Party understands and acknowledges that a representation or warranty of any kind, wh ether express or implied, is made as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information provid ed by either Party, and the Disclosing P arty and its affiliates, Representatives, directors, officers, equity owners, managers, employees or agents s hall have liability to the R ecipient or to any other person or en tity resulting from use of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that 'no representation or warranty of any kind... is made' and that 'neither... shall have any liability.' The changed text reverses this, stating that 'a representation or warranty of any kind... is made' and 'the Disclosing Party... shall have liability.' The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, confirming the contradiction. Therefore, the change creates a direct and strong contradiction regarding liability and warranty, making the original agreement and the changed agreement incompatible and logically inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and its rights and obligations shall not be assignable without the prior written consent of the other party hereto, and any attempted assignment without such consent shall be void; provided , however , that such consent shall not be require d in the case of an assignment to a successor to the business and assets of the assignor, which successor agrees to be bound to the terms of this Agreement and the obligations of the assignor under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and its rights and obligations shall be freely assignable without the prior written consent of the other party hereto, and any attempted assignment without such consent shall not be void; provided , however , that such consent shall be require d in the case of an assignment to a successor to the business and assets of the assignor, which successor does not have to agree to be bound to the terms of this Agreement and the obligations of the assignor under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'not' from 'shall not be assignable' and stating 'shall be freely assignable' and changing 'does not have to agree to be bound to the terms' introduces a contradiction to its original meaning. The change in wording and terms creates in-text contradicition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 22. Successors and Assigns . This Agreement and its rights and obligations shall be freely assignable without the prior written consent of the other party hereto, and any attempted assignment without such consent shall not be void; provided , however , that such consent shall be require d in the case of an assignment to a successor to the business and assets of the assignor, which successor does not have to agree to be bound to the terms of this Agreement and the obligations of the assignor under this Agreement. Subject to the preceding sentence, the pr ovisions of this Agreement shall inure to the benefit of and shall be binding upon the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns. An assigning party shall continue to be bound to all of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The original text stated that the agreement was not assignable without prior written consent. The changed text states that the agreement is 'freely assignable' without consent, directly contradicting the requirement for consent. Additionally, the changed text states that a successor 'does not have to agree to be bound to the terms', which contradicts the original requirement that a successor must agree to be bound. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states the same thing, but is meant to be the original text before change. The changed text therefore causes in-text contradiction.
